image-alt.title: Image missing description
image-alt.description: This image has no alt text. Screen readers can't tell blind visitors what this image shows.

fix.generic: Add a description to this image in your website editor. Describe what the image shows in 1-2 sentences.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: click the image → fill in the 'Alt Text' field in the right sidebar.
fix.squarespace: In Squarespace: click the image → click the pencil icon → fill in 'Image Alt Text'.
fix.google_sites: In Google Sites: click the image → click the three dots → 'Alt text' → enter description.
fix.civicplus: In CivicPlus: edit the page → click the image → fill in the 'Alternate Text' field.
fix.finalsite: In Finalsite Composer: click the image → 'Image Properties' → fill in 'Alt Text'.

color-contrast.title: Text hard to read (low contrast)
color-contrast.description: The text color is too similar to the background color, making it hard to read for people with low vision.

fix.generic: Change the text or background color so there is more contrast between them. Use a contrast checker tool to verify.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: edit the block → change text or background color in the block settings sidebar.
fix.squarespace: In Squarespace: edit the section → adjust colors in the Design panel.
fix.google_sites: In Google Sites: select the text → change the text color from the formatting toolbar.
fix.civicplus: In CivicPlus: edit the page → select the text → adjust colors in the editor toolbar.
fix.finalsite: In Finalsite: edit the element → change colors in the style settings.

link-name.title: Link has no descriptive text
link-name.description: This link has no text that describes where it goes. Screen reader users won't know what this link does.

fix.generic: Add descriptive text to the link. Avoid 'click here' or 'read more' — describe the destination.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: edit the link text in the block editor, or add an aria-label via a custom HTML block.
fix.squarespace: In Squarespace: click the link → edit the link text to be descriptive.
fix.civicplus: In CivicPlus: edit the page → update the link text to describe where it goes.
fix.finalsite: In Finalsite: click the link → update the display text to be descriptive.

html-has-lang.title: Page missing language
html-has-lang.description: The page doesn't specify what language it's written in. Screen readers need this to pronounce words correctly.

fix.generic: Add lang="en" to the <html> tag in your page template.
fix.wordpress: This is usually set by your theme. Check Settings → General → Site Language.
fix.squarespace: Squarespace sets this automatically. If missing, contact Squarespace support.
fix.wix: In Wix: go to Settings → Multilingual and ensure a language is set.
fix.civicplus: Contact CivicPlus support to set the page language attribute in the template.
fix.finalsite: Contact Finalsite support to set the lang attribute in the site template.

label.title: Form field missing label
label.description: This form field (text box, dropdown, etc.) has no label. Screen reader users won't know what to enter.

fix.generic: Add a visible label to every form field. The label should describe what information to enter.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: use a form plugin (like Gravity Forms or WPForms) and ensure each field has a label configured.
fix.squarespace: In Squarespace: edit the form block → click each field → enter a label in the field settings.
fix.civicplus: In CivicPlus: edit the form → click each field → fill in the 'Label' property.
fix.finalsite: In Finalsite: edit the form → ensure each field has a visible label configured.

button-name.title: Button has no label
button-name.description: This button has no text or label. Screen reader users won't know what this button does.

fix.generic: Add text inside the button, or add an aria-label attribute describing the button's action.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: edit the button block → add button text. For icon-only buttons, add aria-label via custom HTML.
fix.squarespace: In Squarespace: click the button → add descriptive button text in the settings.
fix.wix: In Wix: click the button → edit the button text in the panel.
fix.civicplus: In CivicPlus: edit the page → click the button → update the button label.
fix.finalsite: In Finalsite: edit the button element → add descriptive text.

document-title.title: Page has no title
document-title.description: The page is missing a title. Screen readers announce the page title when users navigate to a new page.

fix.generic: Add a <title> tag in the page's <head> section. It should describe the page content.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: edit the page → set the title at the top. WordPress generates the <title> tag.
fix.squarespace: In Squarespace: edit the page → Settings → SEO → set the SEO Title.
fix.wix: In Wix: go to Page SEO settings → enter a page title.
fix.civicplus: In CivicPlus: edit the page → fill in the 'Page Title' or 'SEO Title' field.
fix.finalsite: In Finalsite: edit the page → fill in the 'Browser Title' field in page settings.

heading-order.title: Heading levels skip
heading-order.description: Headings skip a level (e.g., H1 to H3). This makes it harder for screen reader users to understand the page structure.

fix.generic: Use headings in order: H1 → H2 → H3. Don't skip levels for visual styling — use CSS instead.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: in the block editor, select the heading block → change the heading level in the toolbar.
fix.squarespace: In Squarespace: click the text block → use the text style dropdown to set the correct heading level.
fix.wix: In Wix: click the text → use the text theme dropdown to set H1, H2, or H3 in order.
fix.civicplus: In CivicPlus: edit the page → select text → use the heading style dropdown to correct the level.
fix.finalsite: In Finalsite: edit the text element → use the paragraph format selector to choose the correct heading level.

list.title: List not properly structured
list.description: This list isn't using proper HTML list tags. Screen readers may not announce it as a list.

fix.generic: Use <ul> or <ol> tags for lists instead of formatting text with dashes or numbers.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: select the text → click the list icon in the block toolbar to convert to a proper list.
fix.squarespace: In Squarespace: select the text → click the bullet list or numbered list button in the editor.
fix.civicplus: In CivicPlus: select the text → use the list buttons in the editor toolbar.
fix.finalsite: In Finalsite: select the text → use the list formatting buttons in the Composer toolbar.

region.title: Content not in a landmark
region.description: Some page content isn't inside a landmark region (header, nav, main, footer). This makes it harder for screen reader users to navigate.

fix.generic: Wrap page content in semantic HTML elements: <header>, <nav>, <main>, <footer>.
fix.wordpress: Most modern WordPress themes handle this. If using a custom theme, add proper semantic elements.
fix.squarespace: Squarespace templates handle landmark regions. If missing, contact Squarespace support.
fix.civicplus: Contact CivicPlus support to update the page template with proper landmark regions.
fix.finalsite: Contact Finalsite support to ensure the template uses proper semantic landmark elements.

aria-allowed-attr.title: ARIA attribute not allowed on this element
aria-allowed-attr.description: An ARIA attribute is being used on an element that doesn't support it. This can confuse screen readers.

fix.generic: Remove the invalid ARIA attribute or change the element's role to one that supports it. Consult the ARIA specification for allowed attributes per role.

aria-hidden-body.title: Page body is hidden from screen readers
aria-hidden-body.description: The page body has aria-hidden="true", which hides the entire page from screen reader users.

fix.generic: Remove aria-hidden="true" from the <body> element. This attribute should never be placed on the body.

aria-hidden-focus.title: Focusable element is hidden from screen readers
aria-hidden-focus.description: An element that can receive keyboard focus has aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users can navigate to it but won't hear it announced.

fix.generic: Remove aria-hidden="true" from focusable elements (links, buttons, inputs). If you want to hide it from all users, also add tabindex="-1" and visually hide it.

aria-input-field-name.title: ARIA input field has no accessible name
aria-input-field-name.description: A form input with an ARIA role has no label that screen readers can announce.

fix.generic: Add a label using aria-label, aria-labelledby, or a visible <label> element associated with the input.

aria-required-attr.title: Required ARIA attribute missing
aria-required-attr.description: An element with an ARIA role is missing a required ARIA attribute that the role needs to work properly.

fix.generic: Add the missing required ARIA attribute(s) to the element. Check the WAI-ARIA specification for which attributes are required for the element's role.

aria-required-children.title: ARIA parent element missing required child elements
aria-required-children.description: An element with an ARIA role is missing required child elements. Screen readers expect a specific structure.

fix.generic: Add the required child elements inside this ARIA parent. For example, a role="list" element needs children with role="listitem".

aria-required-parent.title: ARIA element missing required parent
aria-required-parent.description: An ARIA element is not inside the required parent element. Screen readers expect a specific structure.

fix.generic: Move this element inside the required parent element. For example, role="listitem" must be inside role="list".

aria-roles.title: Invalid ARIA role
aria-roles.description: An element has an ARIA role that doesn't exist or isn't valid. Screen readers won't be able to interpret it.

fix.generic: Replace the invalid role with a valid ARIA role, or remove the role attribute entirely.

aria-toggle-field-name.title: Toggle field has no accessible name
aria-toggle-field-name.description: A checkbox or radio button has no label that screen readers can announce to users.

fix.generic: Add a label using a <label> element, aria-label, or aria-labelledby attribute.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: use a form plugin and ensure each toggle/checkbox field has a label configured.
fix.civicplus: In CivicPlus: edit the form → add a label for each checkbox or radio button field.

aria-valid-attr.title: Invalid ARIA attribute
aria-valid-attr.description: An ARIA attribute being used doesn't exist in the ARIA specification. Screen readers may ignore or misinterpret it.

fix.generic: Remove or correct the invalid ARIA attribute. Check the WAI-ARIA specification for the correct attribute names.

aria-valid-attr-value.title: ARIA attribute has invalid value
aria-valid-attr-value.description: An ARIA attribute has a value that isn't allowed. This can cause screen readers to behave unexpectedly.

fix.generic: Correct the ARIA attribute value. For example, aria-expanded must be 'true' or 'false', and aria-labelledby must point to an existing element ID.

bypass.title: No way to skip navigation
bypass.description: There's no skip link or other mechanism to bypass repeated navigation. Keyboard users must tab through all navigation items on every page.

fix.generic: Add a 'Skip to main content' link as the first focusable element on the page. It can be visually hidden until focused.
fix.wordpress: Many accessible WordPress themes include skip links. If yours doesn't, add one to your theme's header.php file.
fix.civicplus: Contact CivicPlus support to add a skip navigation link to the site template.
fix.finalsite: Contact Finalsite support to add a skip navigation link to the page template.

definition-list.title: Definition list not properly structured
definition-list.description: A definition list (<dl>) contains elements that aren't proper definition terms (<dt>) or descriptions (<dd>).

fix.generic: Ensure <dl> elements only contain <dt> and <dd> children. Wrap any other content in <div> elements inside the <dl>.

dlitem.title: Definition list item in wrong context
dlitem.description: A <dt> or <dd> element is not inside a <dl> element. Screen readers won't announce this correctly.

fix.generic: Wrap <dt> and <dd> elements inside a <dl> element.

focus-order-semantics.title: Element should not be focusable
focus-order-semantics.description: An element that shouldn't receive keyboard focus has been made focusable. This disrupts the keyboard navigation order.

fix.generic: Remove tabindex from elements that shouldn't receive focus, or use tabindex="-1" if you need to programmatically focus them.

frame-focusable-content.title: Frame with focusable content has no title
frame-focusable-content.description: An iframe that contains interactive content doesn't have a title, so screen reader users don't know what's inside.

fix.generic: Add a title attribute to the <iframe> element that describes its content (e.g., <iframe title="YouTube video: Annual Report 2024">).

frame-title.title: Frame missing title
frame-title.description: An iframe or frame has no title attribute. Screen reader users can't tell what content the frame contains.

fix.generic: Add a descriptive title attribute to every <iframe> element.
fix.wordpress: When embedding content, add title attributes via a Custom HTML block or the embed block's advanced settings.
fix.squarespace: When adding a Code Block with an iframe, include a title attribute in the iframe HTML.
fix.wix: In Wix: when adding an HTML iFrame element, include title in the HTML code.
fix.civicplus: In CivicPlus: when embedding iframes, add a title attribute to the HTML in the page editor.
fix.finalsite: In Finalsite: when adding embedded content, include a descriptive title attribute in the iframe HTML.

frame-title-unique.title: Multiple frames have the same title
frame-title-unique.description: Two or more iframes on the page have identical title attributes. Screen reader users can't distinguish between them.

fix.generic: Give each iframe a unique, descriptive title that identifies the specific content it contains.

html-lang-valid.title: Page language code is invalid
html-lang-valid.description: The page has a lang attribute but the language code isn't valid. Screen readers need a valid code to select the right voice.

fix.generic: Use a valid BCP 47 language code in the lang attribute (e.g., lang="en" for English, lang="es" for Spanish).

html-xml-lang-mismatch.title: HTML lang and xml:lang don't match
html-xml-lang-mismatch.description: The lang and xml:lang attributes on the <html> element specify different languages. This can confuse screen readers.

fix.generic: Ensure the lang and xml:lang attributes have the same value (e.g., both should be lang="en" xml:lang="en").

input-button-name.title: Input button has no accessible name
input-button-name.description: A button created with an <input type="button"> or <input type="submit"> element has no label.

fix.generic: Add a value attribute to the input element that describes the button's action (e.g., value="Submit Form").

input-image-alt.title: Image button missing alt text
input-image-alt.description: A button that uses an image (<input type="image">) has no alt text describing what the button does.

fix.generic: Add an alt attribute to the <input type="image"> element that describes the button's action, not just what the image looks like.

label-title-only.title: Form field labeled only by title attribute
label-title-only.description: A form field's only label is a title attribute (tooltip). This isn't reliable for all screen readers.

fix.generic: Add a visible <label> element or aria-label attribute instead of relying solely on the title attribute.

landmark-banner-is-top-level.title: Banner landmark is not at the top level
landmark-banner-is-top-level.description: The site header (<header> or role="banner") is nested inside another landmark. It should be a top-level landmark.

fix.generic: Move the <header> or banner role element to be a direct child of <body>, not nested inside other landmark elements.

landmark-complementary-is-top-level.title: Complementary landmark is not at the top level
landmark-complementary-is-top-level.description: An aside element or complementary role is nested inside another landmark instead of being a top-level landmark.

fix.generic: Move <aside> elements to be top-level landmarks, or ensure they're nested appropriately for their context.

landmark-contentinfo-is-top-level.title: Footer landmark is not at the top level
landmark-contentinfo-is-top-level.description: The site footer (<footer> or role="contentinfo") is nested inside another landmark instead of being top-level.

fix.generic: Move the <footer> element to be a direct child of <body>, not nested inside other landmark elements.

landmark-main-is-top-level.title: Main landmark is not at the top level
landmark-main-is-top-level.description: The main content area (<main>) is nested inside another landmark instead of being a top-level landmark.

fix.generic: Move the <main> element to be a direct child of <body>, not nested inside other landmark elements.

landmark-no-duplicate-banner.title: Page has more than one banner landmark
landmark-no-duplicate-banner.description: Multiple <header> or banner role elements exist at the top level. There should only be one site-wide banner.

fix.generic: Ensure only one <header> element or role="banner" exists as a top-level landmark on the page.

landmark-no-duplicate-contentinfo.title: Page has more than one contentinfo landmark
landmark-no-duplicate-contentinfo.description: Multiple <footer> or contentinfo role elements exist at the top level. There should only be one site-wide footer.

fix.generic: Ensure only one <footer> element or role="contentinfo" exists as a top-level landmark on the page.

landmark-no-duplicate-main.title: Page has more than one main landmark
landmark-no-duplicate-main.description: Multiple <main> elements exist on the page. There should be only one main content area.

fix.generic: Ensure only one <main> element exists on the page. Remove or replace duplicates with <section> or <div>.

landmark-one-main.title: Page has no main landmark
landmark-one-main.description: The page has no <main> element or role="main" landmark. Screen reader users can't quickly jump to the main content.

fix.generic: Wrap the primary page content in a <main> element.
fix.wordpress: Most WordPress themes include a <main> element. If yours doesn't, update the theme template.
fix.civicplus: Contact CivicPlus support to add a <main> landmark to the page template.
fix.finalsite: Contact Finalsite support to ensure the page template includes a <main> landmark element.

landmark-unique.title: Landmark regions must have unique labels
landmark-unique.description: Multiple landmark regions of the same type exist but have identical labels, making them indistinguishable for screen reader users.

fix.generic: Add unique aria-label or aria-labelledby attributes to distinguish landmark regions of the same type (e.g., aria-label="Main Navigation" and aria-label="Footer Navigation" for two <nav> elements).

link-in-text-block.title: Link not distinguishable from surrounding text
link-in-text-block.description: A link within a paragraph of text is only distinguished by color (no underline). Users with color blindness may not recognize it as a link.

fix.generic: Add an underline to inline links, or use another visual indicator beyond color (e.g., bold, border, icon).
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: add CSS to your theme to underline links within content: a { text-decoration: underline; }
fix.squarespace: In Squarespace: go to Design → Custom CSS and add link underline styles.

listitem.title: List item in wrong context
listitem.description: A <li> element is not inside a <ul> or <ol> element. Screen readers won't announce it as a list item.

fix.generic: Wrap <li> elements inside a <ul> (unordered) or <ol> (ordered) element.

marquee.title: Scrolling marquee text
marquee.description: A <marquee> element creates auto-scrolling text. This can cause problems for users with cognitive disabilities and those who need more time to read.

fix.generic: Remove the <marquee> element and display the content as static text instead.

meta-refresh.title: Page auto-refreshes or redirects
meta-refresh.description: A meta refresh tag automatically redirects or reloads the page. This can disorient screen reader users mid-session.

fix.generic: Remove the <meta http-equiv="refresh"> tag. Use server-side redirects instead for page redirects.

meta-viewport.title: Viewport zoom disabled
meta-viewport.description: The page prevents users from zooming in. This affects users with low vision who need to enlarge content to read it.

fix.generic: Remove user-scalable=no and maximum-scale=1 from the viewport meta tag. Use: <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
fix.wordpress: Check your theme's header.php or functions.php and remove user-scalable=no from the viewport meta tag.
fix.squarespace: Squarespace may inject this. Contact Squarespace support if you cannot control the viewport meta tag.

meta-viewport-large.title: Viewport doesn't scale to large sizes
meta-viewport-large.description: The viewport meta tag limits how much users can zoom in, which can affect users with low vision.

fix.generic: Set maximum-scale to at least 5 in the viewport meta tag, or remove the maximum-scale restriction entirely.

object-alt.title: Embedded object missing alternative text
object-alt.description: An <object> element (used for embedded content like PDFs or Flash) has no alternative text for users whose assistive technology cannot access it.

fix.generic: Add descriptive text inside the <object> element, or add an aria-label attribute describing the embedded content.

p-as-heading.title: Paragraph styled to look like a heading
p-as-heading.description: A paragraph element is styled to visually look like a heading but isn't marked up as one. Screen reader users can't navigate to it as a heading.

fix.generic: Change the paragraph to a proper heading element (<h2>, <h3>, etc.) to match its visual appearance.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: click the text block → change the block type from Paragraph to Heading using the block toolbar.
fix.squarespace: In Squarespace: click the text → use the text style dropdown to select the appropriate heading level.
fix.wix: In Wix: click the text element → use the theme selector to set it as a heading instead of body text.
fix.civicplus: In CivicPlus: select the text → use the heading style selector in the editor toolbar.
fix.finalsite: In Finalsite: select the text → use the paragraph format dropdown to select the appropriate heading level.

page-has-heading-one.title: Page missing a top-level heading
page-has-heading-one.description: The page has no H1 heading. Screen reader users rely on H1 to understand the main topic of the page.

fix.generic: Add a single H1 heading to the page that describes its main content.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: the page title is usually the H1. Ensure your template uses the_title() wrapped in an <h1>.
fix.squarespace: In Squarespace: the page title block typically serves as H1. Check that it's styled as Heading 1.
fix.civicplus: In CivicPlus: ensure the page title is marked as H1 in the template settings.
fix.finalsite: In Finalsite: ensure the page title is configured to output as an H1 element.

role-img-alt.title: Image role element missing label
role-img-alt.description: An element with role="img" has no accessible label. Screen readers can't describe this content to blind users.

fix.generic: Add an aria-label attribute to describe the image content, or add aria-labelledby pointing to a nearby text description.

scope-attr-valid.title: Table scope attribute is invalid
scope-attr-valid.description: A table header's scope attribute has an invalid value. Screen readers use scope to understand table structure.

fix.generic: Set the scope attribute to a valid value: 'row', 'col', 'rowgroup', or 'colgroup'.

scrollable-region-focusable.title: Scrollable area not keyboard accessible
scrollable-region-focusable.description: A scrollable region on the page can't be reached or scrolled with a keyboard. Keyboard users can't access all the content.

fix.generic: Add tabindex="0" to scrollable container elements so keyboard users can focus and scroll them.

select-name.title: Dropdown missing label
select-name.description: A dropdown (<select>) element has no label. Screen reader users won't know what to select.

fix.generic: Add a <label> element associated with the select, or add an aria-label attribute.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: use a form plugin and ensure each dropdown field has a label.
fix.civicplus: In CivicPlus: edit the form → add a label to each dropdown field.
fix.finalsite: In Finalsite: edit the form element → ensure each dropdown has a visible label.

server-side-image-map.title: Server-side image map used
server-side-image-map.description: A server-side image map is used. These are not accessible to keyboard-only users or screen reader users.

fix.generic: Replace server-side image maps with client-side image maps (using <map> and <area> elements with alt text) or use separate links.

skip-link.title: Skip link doesn't work
skip-link.description: A skip navigation link exists but it doesn't point to a valid target on the page, so it doesn't actually skip navigation.

fix.generic: Ensure the skip link's href matches the id of the main content element (e.g., <a href="#main-content"> paired with <main id="main-content">).

tabindex.title: Tabindex value greater than 0
tabindex.description: An element has a tabindex greater than 0, which creates a custom tab order that can be confusing for keyboard users.

fix.generic: Remove positive tabindex values. Use tabindex="0" to make an element focusable in natural DOM order, or reorganize the HTML so elements appear in the logical tab order.

table-duplicate-name.title: Table caption and summary are identical
table-duplicate-name.description: A data table has the same text for its caption and summary. This creates redundant announcements for screen reader users.

fix.generic: Make the caption and summary serve different purposes: use caption for a brief title and summary for a detailed description of the table's structure.

table-fake-caption.title: Data table uses fake caption
table-fake-caption.description: A table uses a regular cell to serve as a caption instead of the proper <caption> element.

fix.generic: Replace the fake caption cell with a proper <caption> element as the first child of <table>.

td-has-header.title: Table cell has no associated header
td-has-header.description: A data cell in a complex table has no associated header cell. Screen readers can't tell users what column or row the data belongs to.

fix.generic: Add proper <th> elements with scope attributes, or use headers and id attributes to associate data cells with their headers.

td-headers-attr.title: Table cell headers attribute is invalid
td-headers-attr.description: A table cell's headers attribute references IDs that don't exist or don't belong to <th> elements.

fix.generic: Ensure the headers attribute on <td> elements contains valid IDs of <th> elements in the same table.

th-has-data-cells.title: Table header has no associated data cells
th-has-data-cells.description: A <th> element doesn't appear to have any data cells that it acts as a header for.

fix.generic: Ensure every <th> element corresponds to data cells. If the element is not truly a header, change it to a <td> element.

valid-lang.title: Language attribute has invalid code
valid-lang.description: An element has a lang attribute but the language code is not valid. Screen readers need valid codes to select the correct speech voice.

fix.generic: Use a valid BCP 47 language code (e.g., lang="fr" for French, lang="de" for German) on any element with a lang attribute.

video-caption.title: Video missing captions
video-caption.description: A video on the page has no captions. Deaf users and those in noisy environments can't access the audio content.

fix.generic: Add closed captions to the video using a <track kind="captions"> element. Provide a WebVTT (.vtt) caption file.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: use a video block → ensure your video file has captions, or add them via YouTube/Vimeo and embed the captioned video.
fix.squarespace: In Squarespace: embed YouTube or Vimeo videos with captions enabled, or add a Video Block with a captioned video.
fix.civicplus: In CivicPlus: embed videos from YouTube or Vimeo with captions enabled, or upload a video with a captions track.
fix.finalsite: In Finalsite: embed captioned videos from YouTube or Vimeo, or add a WebVTT caption file to self-hosted videos.

autocomplete-valid.title: Autocomplete attribute is invalid
autocomplete-valid.description: A form field has an autocomplete attribute with an invalid value. This prevents browsers and password managers from helping users fill in forms.

fix.generic: Use valid HTML autocomplete values such as 'name', 'email', 'tel', 'street-address', 'postal-code', etc.

avoid-inline-spacing.title: Inline text spacing overrides
avoid-inline-spacing.description: Inline styles force text spacing in a way that can't be adjusted by users. Some people need to override text spacing for readability.

fix.generic: Move text spacing CSS (line-height, letter-spacing, word-spacing, margin/padding between lines) out of inline styles and into stylesheets where users can override them.

empty-heading.title: Heading is empty
empty-heading.description: A heading element exists but contains no text. Screen readers will announce it as a heading but with nothing to say.

fix.generic: Add descriptive text to the heading, or remove the empty heading element if it was added unintentionally.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: check your blocks for empty heading blocks and either fill them in or delete them.
fix.squarespace: In Squarespace: check for empty text blocks styled as headings and remove or populate them.
fix.civicplus: In CivicPlus: edit the page and remove any empty heading elements from the content editor.
fix.finalsite: In Finalsite: edit the page and remove or fill in any empty heading elements in Composer.

empty-table-header.title: Table header cell is empty
empty-table-header.description: A <th> element has no text. Screen readers will announce an empty header, which doesn't help users understand the table.

fix.generic: Add descriptive text to the table header cell. If the cell intentionally has no header (e.g., a corner cell), use role="presentation" or add a hidden label.

form-field-multiple-labels.title: Form field has multiple labels
form-field-multiple-labels.description: A form field is associated with more than one label. Screen readers may announce both, which can be confusing.

fix.generic: Remove duplicate label associations. Each form field should have exactly one label.

identical-links-same-purpose.title: Links with same text go to different places
identical-links-same-purpose.description: Multiple links on the page have identical text but go to different destinations, making navigation confusing for screen reader users.

fix.generic: Make each link's text unique and descriptive of its destination. If links must look the same visually, add aria-label attributes to differentiate them.

label-content-name-mismatch.title: Button or link label doesn't match visible text
label-content-name-mismatch.description: The accessible name of a button or link doesn't match its visible text. Voice control users who say the visible text won't activate the right element.

fix.generic: Ensure the aria-label or accessible name begins with or matches the visible text of the element.

nested-interactive.title: Interactive element nested inside another interactive element
nested-interactive.description: A button, link, or other interactive element is nested inside another interactive element. This confuses screen readers and keyboard navigation.

fix.generic: Move the nested interactive element outside of its parent, or restructure the HTML so interactive elements are not nested inside each other.

no-autoplay-audio.title: Audio plays automatically
no-autoplay-audio.description: Audio on the page starts playing automatically. This can interfere with screen readers and startle users.

fix.generic: Remove the autoplay attribute from audio and video elements. Let users choose to play audio themselves.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: check any media blocks or audio widgets and disable autoplay in their settings.
fix.squarespace: In Squarespace: check video or audio blocks and uncheck the 'Autoplay' option.

svg-img-alt.title: SVG image missing accessible label
svg-img-alt.description: An SVG image that conveys information has no accessible label, so screen reader users won't know what it shows.

fix.generic: Add a <title> element as the first child of the <svg>, or add aria-label and role="img" to the <svg> element.

target-size.title: Click target is too small
target-size.description: A clickable element is smaller than 24x24 pixels, making it difficult for users with motor disabilities or tremors to click accurately.

fix.generic: Increase the size of the clickable area to at least 24x24 pixels using padding or min-width/min-height CSS properties.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: add CSS to increase the size of small buttons or links in your theme customizer or stylesheet.
fix.squarespace: In Squarespace: use Custom CSS to increase padding on small interactive elements.

duplicate-id.title: Duplicate ID on page
duplicate-id.description: Two or more elements on the page share the same ID attribute. IDs must be unique — duplicate IDs can break ARIA relationships and form labels.

fix.generic: Ensure every element's id attribute is unique on the page. Rename duplicate IDs to be distinct.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: check for plugins that may output duplicate IDs. Update or replace conflicting plugins.
fix.squarespace: In Squarespace: check for duplicate ID attributes in Code Blocks and custom HTML sections.

duplicate-id-active.title: Duplicate ID on active element
duplicate-id-active.description: Two interactive elements (buttons, inputs, links) share the same ID. ARIA labelledby and form labels may target the wrong element.

fix.generic: Ensure every interactive element has a unique ID. Rename or remove duplicate IDs on focusable elements.

duplicate-id-aria.title: Duplicate ID used in ARIA attribute
duplicate-id-aria.description: An ARIA attribute (like aria-labelledby or aria-describedby) references an ID that belongs to multiple elements.

fix.generic: Make all element IDs unique. When aria-labelledby or aria-describedby references an ID, only one element should have that ID.
